import io
import string
import list

fn find_index(_ nums: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut i = 0
  mut result: Option<Int> = None
  while i < nums.len()
    match result
    when None
      match nums.get(i)
      when Some(n)
        if n == target
          result = Some(i)
        end
      when None
        # no-op
      end
    when Some(_)
      # already found; keep iterating (no break in v0.1)
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Result<Unit, String>
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end

  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end

  let parts = string.split_whitespace(line1)
  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < parts.len()
    match parts.get(i)
    when Some(s)
      match string.parse_int(s)
      when Some(n)
        nums = list.push(nums, n)
      when None
        # skip unparseable token
      end
    when None
      # no-op
    end
    i = i + 1
  end

  let target_str = string.trim(line2)
  let target = match string.parse_int(target_str)
  when Some(n)
    n
  when None
    0
  end

  match find_index(nums, target)
  when Some(idx)
    print("index=#{idx}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end

  Ok(())
end
